concepts.title: Call Bridge (Shared Database Nodes)
concepts.summary: A standalone Node server service that synchronizes state information across ZPLS modules and enables call routing between different ZPLS nodes or node groups during internet outages. Without a Call Bridge, users at different sites cannot call each other when survivability mode is active. Designers should understand this as critical inter-site communication infrastructure that must remain accessible to all ZPLS modules.
key_points: Enables cross-site calls during outages when Zoom cloud is unreachable
Must be deployed on a standalone Node server accessible to all ZPLS modules
Also known as Shared Database Nodes in the UI
Not required for communication between nodes within the same node group

user_roles.title: Account Owner / Administrator
user_roles.summary: Responsible for deploying the Call Bridge service and assigning it to ZPLS modules or node groups. Requires access to both Node Management (for deployment) and Phone System Management (for assignment), meaning designers should ensure consistent navigation patterns between these two admin areas.
key_points: Requires account owner or admin privileges with edit access
Must access both Node Management and Phone System Management interfaces
Task spans two separate admin workflows (deploy and assign)

task_flows.title: Deploy Call Bridge Service
task_flows.summary: A modal-based workflow where admins add the Call Bridge service on a Node server. The flow includes selecting the target Node server, providing the internal IP, and optionally setting an internal domain prefix. The step-by-step dialog approach minimizes confusion for this one-time setup task, but the IP address field requires knowledge of the initial Node server configuration.
key_points: Navigate: Node Management > Modules > Phone Local Survivability > Services tab > Add Services > Call Bridge
Select target Node server, enter internal IP from initial Node configuration
Optional internal domain prefix
Modal dialog guides admin through service addition

task_flows.title: Assign Call Bridge to ZPLS Module or Node Group
task_flows.summary: After deployment, the Call Bridge must be explicitly linked to ZPLS nodes or groups through the Phone System Management interface. The assignment flow ends with a recommendation to manually synchronize ZPLS data, creating a dependency on the sync workflow from KB0085762. The tab-based selection (Node or Node Group) provides clear scope differentiation.
key_points: Navigate: Phone System Management > Company Info > Account Settings > Zoom Node > Local Survivability > Shared database nodes
Click More icon, Edit, then select ZPLS Node or Node Group from corresponding tab
Final step requires manual ZPLS data synchronization (KB0085762 dependency)
Tab-based UI for selecting between Node and Node Group assignment

constraints.title: Call Bridge Network Resiliency Requirements
constraints.summary: The Call Bridge must be deployed on a standalone Node server with connectivity to all ZPLS modules. If the Call Bridge becomes unreachable during survivability mode, cross-site calls fail because the originating node cannot determine the destination node's location. This single-point-of-failure risk should be clearly communicated in the admin UI.
key_points: Standalone Node server required, accessible to all ZPLS modules
Loss of Call Bridge connectivity causes cross-site call failure
Within same node group, nodes communicate without Call Bridge
Network topology resiliency must be considered by admin

constraints.title: ZPLS and Account Prerequisites
constraints.summary: Call Bridge deployment requires Business/Education/Enterprise account, Zoom Node and Zoom Phone subscriptions, admin privileges, and a deployed ZPLS service module. These are the same prerequisites as other ZPLS features, suggesting a unified account eligibility check should be implemented across the admin interface.
key_points: Business, Education, or Enterprise account
Zoom Node and Zoom Phone subscriptions
ZPLS service module must already be deployed
Account owner or admin privileges required

ux_patterns.title: Two-Phase Service Setup Pattern (Deploy then Assign)
ux_patterns.summary: The Call Bridge follows a two-phase setup: first deployment via Node Management (a technical infrastructure step), then assignment via Phone System Management (a business configuration step). This separation of concerns is logical but creates a disjointed experience across two admin areas. Designers should consider whether a unified wizard or post-deployment prompt would improve completion rates.
key_points: Phase 1: Node Management > Modules > Services tab (infrastructure)
Phase 2: Phone System Management > Company Info (business configuration)
Assignment step explicitly prompts manual data sync as final action
Two separate navigation paths; no wizard connecting them
